title: Flecknoe (Bush Hill Farm), Oxford Canal villages

main_route.excerpt: We set off just after 6 for Flecknoe. It's a village east of Leamington Spa. It's on an amazing flat hill: fantastic views from our field on 3 sides of the camping van, including the radio masts at Daventry & a farm at the end of the field.
main_route.notes: Campsite: Bush Hill Farm. Stayed at least Friday 22 June to Sunday 24 June 2001.
Return home inferred; diary ends mid-trip on Sunday morning. mixed_end: true.

excursions.label: Saturday bike ride along Oxford Canal towpath and roads

stops.excerpt: We set off on a bike ride along the canal tow path which was not in good repair. So after that we went along roads. We went through Lower Shuckborough, [?Around] Grandborough, Sawbridge, Wolfhamcote, & back to Flecknoe. Lower Shuckborough has
stops.notes: Has a fine church with double dog-tooth arches and wooden roof; they were let in by a woman doing flowers.

stops.excerpt: At Grandborough we had a good lunch at "the Shoulder of Mutton". Simon had home-baked ham, Eve had scampi Charles had Riche. There was a pond.
We went through Lower Shuckborough, [?Around] Grandborough, Sawbridge, Wolfhamcote, & back to Flecknoe.
stops.notes: Named in route list but no detail given; passed through on bike ride.

stops.excerpt: Wolfhamcote is deserted. The canal had an extra spur there. The fine stone church is closed up.
stops.notes: Deserted medieval village; fine stone church closed up; canal had an extra spur there.

excursions.label: Sunday morning bike ride to Wolfhamcote church along Oxford Canal

stops.excerpt: By 9.30. we were at Wolfhamcote church, where we left the van + cycled off. The church Key is kept in a box at the manor house, so we were able to see inside. It's very beautiful.
stops.notes: Church key kept at manor house; they were able to see inside on Sunday morning. Diary cuts off here.

unresolved.issue: Transcription uncertainty: the word before 'Grandborough' is unclear — possibly 'Around' or a place name. Likely just the word 'around' as a preposition rather than a place.
unresolved.excerpt: We went through Lower Shuckborough, [?Around] Grandborough, Sawbridge, Wolfhamcote, & back to Flecknoe.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0295_R begins with end of previous trip (May 30th return home entry) before this trip starts on Friday June 22nd.
mixed_end: diary cuts off mid-Sunday morning narrative; return date unknown.
summary: A short summer weekend camping trip (Friday 22 – Sunday 24 June 2001) to Bush Hill Farm at Flecknoe, a village on a hilltop east of Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, with fantastic views towards Daventry. Simon, Eve and Charles (William being in Guatemala) spent Saturday on a 10-mile bike ride along the Oxford Canal towpath and local roads through Lower Shuckburgh, Grandborough, Sawbridge and Wolfhamcote, and Sunday morning visited the beautiful medieval church at deserted Wolfhamcote.
extraction_notes: William explicitly absent (in Guatemala). Thomas, Peter, John not mentioned — uncertain whether they attended; only Simon, Eve and Charles are named in the narrative. Tinder the dog (here called 'Tonic' in one spelling — likely same dog) rode in a bike bag and ran on the lead for about 8 miles on Saturday. The page A_0295_R begins mid-sentence with the end of a previous trip; this trip starts at 'Friday June 22nd.' The diary cuts off mid-Sunday morning. The glued-in Oxford Canal pictorial map postcard on A_0296_R is a souvenir, not a hand-drawn route sketch. No crossings — entirely domestic UK trip.
